mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 55% accuracy over a 2-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation signals supply constraints and margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods produce positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly federal funds rate hike exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero Rank IC with 60-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or predictive accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth confirms robust aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns with > 55% accuracy over a 3-year out-of-sample period, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side bottlenecks that erode corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or the mechanism shows zero directional predictability.
